seo.title: Denis Kukobin — QA Lead | Senior QA Engineer
seo.description: Denis Kukobin — QA Lead with 6+ years of experience. iGaming platform testing, Playwright automation, microservices Go/Kafka/K8s. Senior QA engineer portfolio.

description.label: Who I am as a dev

title.name: QA Engineer
title.headline: QA Lead — I find what breaks the system before the users do.

description.body: Six years in QA — still genuinely curious. Started as a React developer, so code doesn't scare me: I read pull requests and catch bugs at the requirements stage. Right now I lead QA at SprutGaming — an iGaming B2B platform: 21 microservices, Go, Kafka, Kubernetes. Team of 14. I introduced AI-assisted testing as a company standard — not as a buzzword, but because it actually works.

items: Came to QA from frontend (React, Next.js) — I understand how a product is built, not just how it looks from the outside.
Four years at Aurora Infinity: blockchain DEX, crypto exchanges, LLM chat — you learn to test things no textbook covers.
Then iGaming: 21 microservices, payment integrations, Go services, Kafka, K8s. Different scale, same mindset.
Introduced AI-assisted QA as a real team workflow: LLM migration verification, Cursor IDE + Claude + Figma MCP — not a pilot, now a standard.
Built QA from scratch: from no documentation to full coverage, DoD, and shift-left processes embedded in the company.

items.detail: First QA role in a company that had none before. White-box testing of a CRM, frontend in React alongside. Ended up as the sole QA on the project.
Several non-standard products back to back: an author social network, an L2 ETH DEX, a crypto platform. Playwright, Jest, ~70% coverage, mobile testing via TestFlight.
LLM chat with MCP servers and agent tool calls — a product with no existing testing playbooks. Wrote the approaches from scratch.
iGaming B2B platform: white-label casinos, payment flows (crypto invoice, H2H, fiat), 21 microservices. Introduced AI-assisted QA as a team workflow.
Team of 14 (9 core + 5 contractors). Built processes from zero, onboarded 8+ engineers, syncs with CTO 3× a week.

items: Make AI-assisted QA the default — not an exotic experiment for a few teams, but a baseline.
Shift-left into culture: quality built in at the requirements stage, not fixed with patches.
Grow people who think in risks, not just metrics.
Cut escaped defects and MTTR through observability and automation.
Give back: articles, standards, talks — because the QA community deserves to grow.

items.title: AI-assisted QA — now a company regulation
items.sub: LLM-based DB migration verification, pixel-perfect via AI, e2e with Cursor IDE + Claude and Figma MCP. Not a pilot — the team's actual workflow.

items.title: QA from scratch: chaos → company standard
items.sub: Day one: no test cases, no docs. Built checklists → test cases → AC review → smoke + e2e + DoD. Adopted across the whole company.

items.title: Team of 14 from zero — and everyone stayed
items.sub: Onboarded 8+ engineers (all still with the company). Led 9 core QA + 5 contractors, daily prioritization, C-level reporting.

items.title: LLM chat: testing where no playbook exists
items.sub: Streaming AI responses, MCP servers, agent tool calls, crypto subscriptions. Wrote the testing approaches for this product class from scratch.

attachments.intro: What usually ships as files: recent QA writing and direct contacts.

title.headline: I ship products from idea to production. Frontend-first, everything else as needed.

description.body: Started as a React frontend developer — code came before anything else. Over my career I've shipped 8+ products of varying complexity: social networks, a DEX on L2 Ethereum, an LLM chat with MCP agents before it was mainstream, and internal tools teams actually use. I know how to take a product to production, not just to a pull request.

projects.name: Papiny Skazki
projects.desc: Bedtime story service with AI generation: every day the neural network created a new story from a short description. Next.js, Supabase, deployed to production.

projects.name: LLM Chat + MCP Agents
projects.desc: LLM chat with support for external MCP servers and custom agent creation. Built in 2024 — before agentic AI became mainstream.

projects.name: DEX on L2 Ethereum
projects.desc: Token swap platform on a custom Layer 2 blockchain. Full cycle: UI, smart contract integration, deployment.

projects.name: Sprint Board
projects.desc: Team dashboard with Jira API: sprint progress, metrics and workload in real time. Adopted by the entire company.

items.message: feat: first job — React frontend @ VegaAvangard
items.detail: CRM system on React. First commercial production, first real deadline — survived both.
DEX on L2 Ethereum, crypto investor social network, writers media platform, LLM chat with MCP agents. Different stacks, one principle — ship to production.

items.message: feat: internal tooling + AI pipelines @ SprutGaming
items.detail: Internal tools for the team, AI pipelines, automation. Sprint Board became the company's standard working tool.
Portfolio on Next.js 16 + MDX. AI bedtime story service. Going public as a tech leader.

items.name: LLM agents and MCP — before the mainstream hype (2024)
